description: sdd-fleet SDD gates: block ALL non-.sdd writes until the spec is FINALIZED (or, in the bug lane, all non-.sdd/non-tests writes until diagnosis.md is CONFIRMED + a reproducing test exists), refuse a spec.md flip to STATUS=FINALIZED unless the current review cycle is approved and blocker-free AND the spec has decidable acceptance criteria (the finalize gate, with the testability floor folded in — TIER=trivial waives REVIEW; a live ESCALATION.md halts the flip; the source-unlock consequence lives in code, not command prose), freeze the qa-authored test suite during a forward feature's BUILD once it is locked (PROGRESS.md TESTS_LOCKED, set after SDD_FLEET_QA_TESTS_READY) so the coder cannot edit the tests it is judged against (the write-lock gate; tests/ stays writable during qa authoring and throughout the bug lane), refuse a forward-feature BUILD source write until TEST_PLAN.md maps every acceptance criterion (AC-<n>) to a test row or records it under ## Gaps (the traceability gate — tests-first, every AC mapped before implementation; inert when the spec carries no AC ids), guard the Bash escape hatch against write-to-source commands while source is locked, confine reviewers to .sdd/ during non-workflow REVIEW/CHANGE_REVIEW (workflow paths skip via .workflow-in-flight marker), block spec'ing a story whose governing epic is not ratified (estate fan-out gate — resolves the superproject via git, reads the epic's RATIFICATION.md; inert for standalone repos), validate spec STATUS + required sections, validate product backlog STATUS + structure, validate bug-lane diagnosis.md STATUS + required sections, require reviewer REVIEW.md entries on non-workflow paths, refuse Stop on failing tests while a feature is active (bounded: escalates to ESCALATION.md after 3 consecutive red stops), reap orphaned .workflow-in-flight markers on Stop, validate service.json against the descriptor schema on write, block a PROGRESS.md→HANDOFF transition whose diff introduces a cross-service edge not declared in service.json consumes[] (the source-scan dependency gate; inert for standalone/non-git repos), force a human gate at the PROGRESS.md->HANDOFF transition when a change's blast radius is risky (>= N transitive consumers, or money_movement/pii on a reached consumer or on the changed service's own descriptor — the blast-radius human gate; the non-forgeable approval path is a follow-up, so until then a risky change halts at HANDOFF, fail-closed), block a PROGRESS.md→HANDOFF transition without a recorded, signature-fresh counterfactual verdict of pass — or the one deliberate skip, no-source-change — from scripts/counterfactual.sh via counterfactual-record.sh (the counterfactual gate: the record is pinned to a content digest of every non-.sdd tracked+untracked file, so a later source/tests edit stales it and re-blocks while a commit of identical content does not; forward lane only, inert without git — the standalone boundary), block a PROGRESS.md→HANDOFF transition on a failing or untraceable suite — TEST_PLAN.md must still map every AC-<n> (or record it under ## Gaps) at flip time AND a recorded, signature-fresh green run from scripts/suite-record.sh must exist; red, skip (no recognized test command), stale, or missing records block (the handoff suite gate; forward lane only, inert without git), block a registry/<contract>/<semver>.json publish before the active feature reaches HANDOFF (the publish-ordering gate — publish is downstream of the human-approved handoff), refuse to overwrite an already-published registry/<contract>/<semver>.json (the append-only/immutability gate — recovery is forward-only: bump the version, never mutate a published one), and block a registry/<contract>/<semver>.json publish that violates any registered consumer expectation (the consumer-driven contract gate), and enforce link discipline on a .sdd/**/*.md write — block any [[wikilink]] at every tier and, in a repo-level .sdd/ doc (member submodule or standalone repo), block a relative link that resolves outside the repo root; the workspace/superproject tier (a .gitmodules at the root) skips the repo-root check so vault down-links into submodules stay legal, and any non-.sdd or non-.md write is inert (the link-discipline gate).
